judgments.critique: not an important hook. I don't care about it

jumps all over the place. uses terms like neocortex/limbic without explanation.
Sentences don't lead into one another, they seem unrelated.
"It speaks to how we truly decide" is vague and not easily understandable
"communicate from the inside out" is meaningless
Its just listing individual statements, they don't support each other. There is no flow and viewer gets lost
"Make it real, let it guide you." adds no value

- has curiosity gap but could be more casual
"Why do people stay loyal to some brands even when better products exist?”
The main point is confusing " people don’t buy **what** you do, they buy **why** you do it.", instantly click away. It needs to be introduced in a clear manner.
not very actionable
"you need to arouse in them an eager want." wording is not clear

- easy to understand story
- focus on other people's incentives
- could be improved by having more setup, more conversational tone
"Shift your pitch, not just your words." is a cheesy ending

- people want to be more efficient/effective in studying
- creates knowledge gap
- adds suspense by saying they sound strange

- clear 3 step structure
- concept => explanation is very clear
- question => answer is a good transition

- uses concrete statistics
- " Studies have found" is good

- "Today I want to reveal to you the secret to getting better at talking to people." people want to be more social
- "you were not born socially handicapped": creates curiosity gap

- "you can take a baby and put him or her in a room full of people" clear example to debunk hook, good setup
-  "The reason your social skills aren't that great is simply because": identify problem, giving insight
- "The secret to getting better at talking to people or learning any language is to simply get more practic" is actionable advice

- " can start feeling motivated all of the time" people want to feel motivated, common problem. No need to explicitly state it, this implicitly makes the audience care
- "they found that the best way to do so is by simply solving problems in your own life": easy to understand, insightful

- "I'll give you an example just to make things more clear." setup for a clear example
- conflict => explore => action => resolution => learning
- easy to follow storyline
- "Build up that belief that you are in control of your destiny" good way to wrap it up, makes user feel like they have agency
- not interesting

- "distorted behavior" is awkward phrasing
- how does flattery and appreciation link to being valued? The way it was introduced is not smooth
- Charles Schwab story came out of nowhere with no good setup
- No storytelling arc, jumps from action to solution. there is no conflict/struggle/learning
not actionable

- too short
- not relatable, takes too long to think of a situation
- An improvement would be: “Why do people repeat the same mistakes even after you criticize them?"

- The story of al capone has no setup, its completely unrelated to being defensive. Doesn't add value
- Makes statements, but doesn't explain how/why it works. Empty statements with no explanation
"Empathy builds bridges, not walls." this adds no value. too abstract and cheesy
"It's time to become the captain of your ship": analogy is too sudden, instant cheese and cringe

- "captain of your ship" analogy is offputting
- each statement can flow into each other more smoothly
- set a goal for your life is very vague

- "You're not going to be successful in life if you become a slave of these technologies" strong statement
- "how does one take back control and make these machines into your slaves instead of the other way around?" invokes thought and curiosity
- people are always scrolling, this targets insecurity and need to be more productive

- "There's basically three things that you can do." clear 3 step process
- States a tip and follows up with explanation
- has clear actionable items in each step

- "Asking out your crush is best done with a simple three-step process" targets emotion of rejection and love
- simplify daunting process in 3 steps

- clear 3 step framework
- Great transition between each step " Once you have their attention, you can then move on to step number two". Conversational and easy to follow along

- actionable steps you can take in real life and follow
- "Pain lights a fire within you so you start taking action" inspiring
- people want money with low barrier of entry, low effort

- "The first career on my list is": clear list
- starts with position and explains what it does. Concept => explanation pattern
- Casual and conversational tone makes it easy to understand
- provides information for users to get jobs. 3 real positions
- "I want to go over the most common forms of meditation": everyone knows meditation is beneficial

- "How does it work? Well, basically you focus on the sensation of breathing": beginner friendly, easy to understand
- "What sort of benefits does this form of meditation bring?" Natural transition into the next topic
- "Follow us for more helpful tips" is not helpful
"top three ways you can boost your testosterone": people want energy

- clear 3 concept structure
- "REM stage of sleep. This means that anything" great example of stating concept and immediately explaning
- "A study conducted in 1983 found that testosterone levels of men increased by 21%" clear evidence that supports the hook promise
- each step is actionable

- "double my productivity" people want to be productive
- "And I was able to do this by implementing five simple, proven tricks": research backed promise

- easy to understand structure
- states tip and follows up with why/how it works
each step is actionable on its own
- " fall asleep within 10 to 15 minutes of going to bed." people want to fall asleep fast, good health

- clear steps to follow: first, second, third
- "producing melatonin. It's basically tricking our body into thinking that it's still the morning" follows up concept with explanation
- each step is actionable
- vague/abstract, not interesting, no value or promise provided
- too abstract, art has no concrete example to life
- no clear action user can take
- vague/abstract, not interesting, no value or promise provided
- too abstract, building a house has no concrete example to life, hard to relate
- no clear action user can take
